description_long: This object is a large, flat solar energy installation featuring multiple rectangular solar panels mounted on a broad, platform-like base. The panels are arranged in a staggered, slightly tilted configuration, with some panels elevated higher than others, suggesting optimization for sunlight capture. The base appears to be constructed from a combination of metal and composite materials, with visible support structures and possible access walkways. The surface is predominantly a mix of dark and light gray tones, with some blue-tinted reflective areas, likely representing the photovoltaic cells. The installation includes several raised black modules, which may house electrical components or serve as mounting points. The overall design is robust and industrial, suitable for placement on rooftops or as a ground-mounted array in an urban environment.
description: A large, flat solar energy installation with multiple staggered, tilted solar panels on a robust platform, featuring gray and blue surfaces and several raised black modules.
description_view_0: From this view, the staggered arrangement and varying heights of the solar panels are most visible, along with the broad, platform-like base.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the tilt angles of the solar panels and the thickness of the supporting base structure.
description_view_2: From the rear, the flat expanse of the base and the alignment of the panels are clear, with less emphasis on the panel tilt.
description_view_3: This side view shows the opposite tilt direction of the panels and the distribution of raised modules across the base.
attribute_panel_orientation: Panels are tilted at approximately 25-30 degrees, facing a consistent direction, with a fixed orientation.
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted on a broad, platform-like base with visible metal support frames.
attribute_array_configuration: Multiple rows of panels in a staggered, linear arrangement, approximately 2-3 rows with 2-3 panels each.
